name: Albuquerque (United States)

colors.symbolism: the Zia sun, the city name and the year of foundation

description: A red field with a yellow Zia sun symbol enclosing the year "1706" in the centre, the word "Albuquerque" written in yellow italics just below the sun, and a yellow flying thunderbird in the canton.
history: The idea of a city flag was first discussed by the Albuquerque Chamber of Commerce in 1967, which announced a design contest running from December 1967 to March 1968. The winning entry, by local optometrist Richard P. Vann, was first flown on 21 June 1968.

features.symbolism: the red field of the flag

features.role: Zuni-style flying thunderbird in the canton
features.symbolism: a stylised yellow thunderbird in the upper hoist, an emblem drawn from Pueblo iconography that situates the city in its Native and Southwestern context

the Zia sun — the same emblem that appears on the New Mexico state flag, drawn from the Zia Pueblo's traditional sun-of-four-directions; here it encloses the year 1706, the year Albuquerque was founded by Provincial Governor Francisco Cuervo y Valdés

features.role: the year "1706" inside the Zia sun, and the word "Albuquerque" in italics below it
features.symbolism: 1706 is the year of the city's founding; "Albuquerque" honours Francisco Fernández de la Cueva, 10th Duke of Alburquerque, then Viceroy of New Spain (the spelling lost the first "r" through usage)
